mission.title: opencost: OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which is billed differently than EC2
mission.description: OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which is billed differently than EC2. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current opencost deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opencost version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
helm list -n opencost 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opencost installation.

steps.title: Review opencost configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost
```
The cost allocation for nodes launched in Fargate mode on AWS can't be correct.
OpenCost should support the "launch type" fargate as it supports the spot instances.

steps.title: Apply the fix for OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which…
steps.description:
I've opened a PR for better support of Fargate nodes pricing calculation at #3350.
I've also published snapshot docker images at [ghcr.io/motoki317/opencost](https://github.com/motoki317/opencost/pkgs/container/opencost), so if anyone is interested in trying this out, you can use this pre-built
```yaml
opencost:
  exporter:
    image:
      repository: motoki317/opencost
      tag: patch-eks-fargate # or "patch-eks"
      pullPolicy: Always
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "OpenCost should support the AWS Fargate launch type which…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I've opened a PR for better support of Fargate nodes pricing calculation at #3350.
I've also published snapshot docker images at [ghcr.io/motoki317/opencost](https://github.com/motoki317/opencost/pkgs/container/opencost), so if anyone is interested in trying this out, you can use this pre-built image.
codeSnippets:
opencost:
  exporter:
    image:
      repository: motoki317/opencost
      tag: patch-eks-fargate # or "patch-eks"
      pullPolicy: Always

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible.
